comments.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
comments.text:
📌 Take a trip into the heart of mystery. 
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QjE0z9xXQ7g&list=PLPsFFj_F_V2ESDcWWCFwfHwlojUqv2I8E&index=1
The Planet Journal connects you with the world's most amazing untold stories. Join our family of travelers today—your next destination is right around the corner.

comments.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
comments.text: *Imagine being given a comfortable life in Britain and still choosing to return to a tiny island beneath a volcano. That raises a bigger question: is home a place, or the people who make you feel you belong? What would you choose?*

comments.author: @hildehausikujohannes7664
comments.text: This is beautiful

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@hildehausikujohannes7664
Thank you! Tristan da Cunha is truly a unique and stunning place. We're glad you enjoyed the documentary!

comments.author: @TripTravel92
comments.text: To anyone reading this, you are not alone, you deserve happiness and everything is aligning perfectly for you. You are exactly where you need to be at this moment

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you so much for sharing such a beautiful and uplifting sentiment. It’s a wonderful reminder for our community, especially as we explore the most remote corners of the world together. We truly appreciate the positive energy you're bringing to The Planet Journal!

comments.author: @TuyetPham-n6t
comments.text: I love the hand for hand community spirit. We really need more of that kind of neighborly love in the modern world.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for watching! The 'hand for hand' system is truly the heart of Tristan da Cunha. It’s a powerful reminder that supporting one another is what builds a strong community, especially in today's world. Glad you enjoyed the documentary!

comments.author: @AmazingDiscoveriesTopUS
comments.text: Most people dream of escaping isolation. The people of Tristan da Cunha chose it. That's hard to understand.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your thoughts! It is indeed a unique perspective. For the residents, isolation isn't a burden, but a choice for peace and a tight-knit community that is rare in our modern, fast-paced world.

comments.author: @WorldExplorationTV
comments.text: So gôd

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you so much! I’m glad you enjoyed this look into life at the end of the earth. Stay tuned for more documentaries!

comments.author: @donnaradu9398
comments.text: The AI’s putting emphasis on wrong words in the script is annoying.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for the honest feedback! I'll pay closer attention to the intonation and emphasis in future documentaries to ensure a better viewing experience. Stay tuned!

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: The 'hand for hand' spirit is truly inspiring. It shows how human connection can overcome even the harshest isolation.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for your thoughtful comment! You’re absolutely right—in a place as remote as Tristan da Cunha, that sense of community isn't just inspiring, it's essential for survival. It’s truly the heart of the island.

comments.author: @rajimac
comments.text: How did they get on during covid? Did Covid reach them?

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@rajimac
Thanks for asking! It’s one of the few places on Earth that stayed completely safe. Glad you enjoyed the documentary!

comments.author: @truehorizons66
comments.text: Tristan da Cunha feels like one of the most remote and fascinating places on Earth. Daily life there must be so different from anywhere else in the world.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for watching! You're absolutely right—the isolation there is profound. It creates a unique lifestyle centered on community and self-sufficiency that is truly hard to find anywhere else in our modern world. Glad you enjoyed the documentary!

comments.author: @chipparker3950
comments.text: AI ?

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thanks for the comment! It's a 100% original production by The Planet Journal team. We appreciate your curiosity!

comments.author: @75jdubb
comments.text: I wonder how they make money and what currency they use.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@75jdubb 
Thank you for watching! The island's economy mainly relies on the export of rock lobsters (crayfish) and the sale of unique postage stamps and coins to collectors worldwide. For currency, they use the British Pound Sterling (£)

comments.author: @PaisajesImposibles-k8c
comments.text: Después de ver este documental, ya entendí por qué dicen que Tristan da Cunha está en el fin del mundo. 😂 Si algún día voy, haré una lista enorme antes de salir de casa, porque regresar por algo olvidado no parece una opción. Aun así, la tranquilidad, la naturaleza y la forma en que vive la comunidad hacen que este lugar tenga un encanto único. Definitivamente es uno de esos destinos que despiertan muchísima curiosidad.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: ¡Gracias por tu estupendo comentario! Tienes toda la razón: ¡olvidar el cepillo de dientes es un gran problema cuando la tienda más cercana está a una semana de viaje en barco! 😂 Es precisamente ese aislamiento extremo lo que preserva la increíble paz y el espíritu comunitario de la isla. ¡Nos alegra mucho que el documental haya despertado tu curiosidad por este rincón único del mundo!

comments.author: @MrJMaton
comments.text: What a scam! A lot of the footage is of somewhere else.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for your feedback. We strive for authenticity in all our documentaries. Thanks for watching!

comments.author: @billmasen3923
comments.text: very little footage of the actual island

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@CtrlCurmudgeon
Thank you! We really wanted to capture the sheer scale and isolation of Tristan da Cunha through those wide perspectives. Glad you noticed and enjoyed the cinematography!

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@billmasen3923
Thank you for your feedback! Due to the extreme remoteness of Tristan da Cunha and strict local regulations regarding filming and privacy, capturing extensive footage is quite challenging. We tried our best to document what we could and will strive to improve the visual variety in our future documentaries.

replies.author: @CtrlCurmudgeon
replies.text: There were literal wide and aerial shots of the island.

comments.author: @indianjoe52
comments.text: That social event reminded me of the film, THE WICKER MAN........

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: It really does have that eerie “everyone knows everyone” atmosphere from *The Wicker Man*. The difference is that on Tristan da Cunha, these traditions seem to come more from generations of isolation and community life than from anything sinister. It makes me wonder how much our sense of normal depends on the kind of society we grow up in. Would you find that close-knit lifestyle comforting or unsettling?

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Interesting comparison! Thanks for sharing your thoughts!

comments.author: @gulfstream8787
comments.text: 12 minutes in and still waiting for the daily life on Tristan da Cunha

replies.author: @CtrlCurmudgeon
replies.text: ​@gulfstream8787 that's one brain cell more than you.

replies.author: @gulfstream8787
replies.text: @CtrlCurmudgeon that’s rather stupid- I was waiting to watch the daily life of the denizens- and not the extended intro into geography. Surely your 2 brain cells could differentiate between the two.

replies.author: @CtrlCurmudgeon
replies.text: Then watch shorts if you have the attention span of a squirrel.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@gulfstream8787 
Thank you for watching and for your feedback! We wanted to provide the necessary context regarding the island's extreme isolation and history first. The deep dive into the residents' daily routines begins right after this segment. We hope you find the rest of the documentary worth the wait!

comments.author: @usnaviartstudio1791
comments.text: The genetic diversity is really low.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@usnaviartstudio1791 
That is a very sharp observation! Due to its extreme isolation and a population of only about 250 people, the island has been a unique subject of study for geneticists for decades. Thank you for adding that insightful point to the conversation!

comments.author: @NomadicCorner
comments.text: Life here must be peaceful and unique

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@NomadicCorner 
Thank you! It truly is a one-of-a-kind place where time seems to slow down. Glad you enjoyed this glimpse into life at the edge of the world!
